title: There is a lot of unnecessary `IdString` refcount churn
body:
`IdString` is refcounted (unless refcounting is disabled, but that creates its own problems). There are a *lot* of places where refcounts are unnecessarily incremented/decremented. This creates some runtime overhead due to touching the memory holding the refcount, and also extra code bloat. Looking at the generated code, the decrement path is especially bloaty.

A lot of functions (around 200?) take one or more parameters of type `IdString`. I think these parameters should be treated in one of two ways:
* If the function actually needs an owning reference to the `IdString`, e.g. because directly or indirectly it stores the `IdString` in a field of an object, then leave the parameter as type `IdString` and use `std::move()` to pass it to another function that takes an owning reference, or when assigning it into a field.
* Otherwise, when the function doesn't need an owning reference but only needs to refer to the `IdString` during the function call, the parameter type should be `const IdString &`.

Both of these changes reduce refcount churn.

For example, [Module::addAnd()](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/d5053033e4ab8733f4c77becac480eca3122ee25/kernel/rtlil.h#L1819) takes an `IdString`, then [passes it](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/d5053033e4ab8733f4c77becac480eca3122ee25/kernel/rtlil.cc#L3158) to `Module::addCell()` which also takes an `IdString`, which then [assigns it](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/d5053033e4ab8733f4c77becac480eca3122ee25/kernel/rtlil.cc#L3069) to `cell->name` without using `std::move()`. So there are two unnecessary increments and decrements along this path that are easy to remove by using `std::move()` at the call to `addCell()` and the assignment to `cell->name`.

Well actually the latter one won't help until we add `IdString::operator=(IdString &&)`, but that's easy.
